review_notes: The functions are generally well-aligned with the NIST 800-171 control 3.1.6, focusing on the use of non-privileged accounts or roles. The naming conventions mostly follow the established pattern, but a slight modification was made to improve clarity and specificity regarding the restriction of service account roles.
naming_improvements: Consider using 'non_privileged_roles' instead of 'restricted_roles' to better convey the intent of using non-privileged roles.
title: 3_1_6: 3.1.6 Use non-privileged accounts or roles when accessing nonsecurity functions
step1_reasoning: The control 3.1.6 focuses on using non-privileged accounts or roles for accessing non-security functions. In Kubernetes, this translates to ensuring that no service accounts or users are bound to the cluster-admin role unless absolutely necessary, enforcing least privilege by assigning the minimal set of permissions required for a task, and ensuring that service accounts are restricted to roles that do not have administrative privileges.
